title: Veterans Member Business Loan Act
plain_summary: This bill helps veterans get business loans from credit unions. Right now, credit unions can only make a certain amount of business loans before they hit their limit. Under this new law, business loans made to veterans will not count toward that limit. This means credit unions can make more business loans to veterans without worrying about going over their allowed amount. The law will start working six months after it is passed.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Veterans Member Business Loan Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Member Business Loan Definition
sections.text: This section changes the Federal Credit Union Act to help veterans get business loans from credit unions. Credit unions have limits on how many business loans they can make to their members. This change says that loans made to veterans do not count toward those limits. A veteran is defined the same way as in title thirty eight of the United States Code, which covers veterans' benefits and services. These changes will start working six months after this law is signed.
